title: Seeded Simulation, Input Replay, State Hashes, Settings, and Save Migration · Guided Program Studio
summary: A two-person studio incrementally builds reproducible replay and versioned local state from original geometric fixtures while retaining earlier Python, repository, determinism, frame-budget, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of reproducible replay and versioned local state while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Inject separate seeded random streams for gameplay and cosmetic effects and record draw-order compatibility.
Record simulation-step actions, seed, version, settings, viewport policy, and checkpoints rather than device events alone.
Canonicalize relevant simulation state and compare tolerant numeric fields without including display-only noise.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rng Streams
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Asteroids team handles case 2567 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject separate seeded random streams for gameplay and cosmetic effects and record draw-order compatibility.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rng Streams.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Rng Streams, keep Rng Streams connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-schema-migration-14sqni
# Competency: asteroids-schema-migration.
# Case 6105: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_schema_migration_14sqni(seed: int = 4217, input_frames: int = 3600, replay_frames: int = 3600, state_hash_equal: bool = True, schema_version: int = 2, corrupted_save_rejected: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_14sqni = "14sqni-inqs41-6105"
    assert evidence_variant_14sqni
    evidence_axes_14sqni = (
        "player14sqni",
        "seed6105",
        "inputinqs41",
        "frame14sqni6105",
        "state610514sqni",
        "playtestinqs416105",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_14sqni)) == 6
    if seed < 0 or input_frames < 1:
        return False, input_frames, "replay-domain"
    if replay_frames != input_frames:
        return False, replay_frames, "replay-length"
    if not state_hash_equal:
        return False, replay_frames, "simulation-drift"
    if schema_version < 1 or not corrupted_save_rejected:
        return False, schema_version, "save-contract"
    assert replay_frames == input_frames
    return True, schema_version, "reproducible-run"
